entry.title: Re: Data Transpose in SAP Datasphere using Union Operation
entry.content:
Datasphere allows us to merge the outputs of 12 nodes using a single union operation. This streamlined approach eliminates the need for multiple unions, simplifying the view while ensuring that all node outputs are consolidated into one cohesive view.

Thanks,

AS

entry.title: Re: How to fix OOMKilled restart issue with SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Duc,

I've updated the post. Hope it clarifies your comments.

Yes, it requires changing the content in case of Tomcat upgrade.

Thanks,

Yannick

entry.title: Re: how to delete an overdue task in successfactors under...
entry.content:
Hi

If it`s only this task, try this lenk to delete the task. 

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3085203

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori RAP display [object Object]
entry.content: You are correct. Thank you so much!

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori launchpad: Hiding empty spaces and pages
entry.content: Is this also going to be enabled for SAP Build Workzone, Standard edition?

entry.title: How to read and parse SAMLResponse in custom Odata read call
entry.content:
Hello All,

Can you please suggest if SAML Response can be read in a custom Odata Read call in below scenario.

1. When user logs in to the portal, SSO authentication is done. During this authentication process, the logged in user is changed based on the configuration in USREXTID table. For example, if user A is maintained as substitute of user B, then user A will access all applications as user B. This is as per SAML configurations.

2. After authentication, while loading the landing page in Fiori there is a Odata call to backend to fetch user attributes. In this call, we are able to fetch attributes of only UserB (as per above configuration). But in this call, we also want to read the actual user ID who is accessing the application. Is it possible to read SAML response or NameID attribute in odata read call.

3. We do not want to read the data from table USREXTID, because same user can have multiple substitute users. Hence, we want to know if its possible to read and parse SAMLresponse.

Best Regards!

entry.title: Re: How to fix OOMKilled restart issue with SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
Thanks @YannickRobin for quick response. Now all questions are well explained.

Duc.

entry.title: Re: 🧰👷‍♂️ The SAP S/4HANA RISE & SAP BTP - Toolbox 👷‍♂️🧰
entry.content:
Joule Agents continued, so this Comment is going be the container for the deep dive in to how we manage and administer and configure Joule Agents....

The AI Copilot Joule | Artificial Intelligence | SAP

Joule AI Agents | Collaborative AI Agents | SAP

The Joule Studio Agent Builder in Joule Studio - Demo

SAP Joule Studio in SAP Build Joule Studio in SAP Build

SAP Discovery Center SAP Build, Joule Studio SAP Discovery Center AI Feature - SAP Build, Joule studio

And this is the doco we have been looking for help.sap.com SAP Build SAP Build, Joule studio  SAP Build | SAP Help Portal   -  but as of 24.02.2025 there's nothing there on the SAP Build, Joule Studio, let's watch

How SAP Uniquely Delivers AI Agents with Joule | SAP News Center

this introduces SAP Knowledge Graph in to the show....

 

Joule AI Agents | Collaborative AI Agents | SAP

 

Agent Builder in Joule Studio - Demo

We need to watch this space for more documentation...

Joule | SAP Help Portal

9d8c0ef2443b49f993a27de8f38f83af.pdf

Initial Setup | SAP Help Portal

Joule Onboarding Guide | SAP Help Portal

Regards,

Sreenivasula Reddy

entry.title: How to see the PR history I approved?
entry.content:
Dear sap,

I want know where can I check the PR lists I approved before? Because in "My Inbox" app, I can only see the PR waiting my approval. but can't see any history I approved.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: The Power of Joule for Developers, ABAP AI Capabilities
entry.content:
@Bruno_Ribeiro  @AlexanderRother : facing the same issue here:
Having a running ABAP environment in BTP, updated ADT for Eclipse, additional licence "8019124 - Joule for Developer Limited Promotion, ABAP" activated (more than 48 hours), role SAP_A4C_BC_DEV_AIQ_PC assigned to Business user - but eclipse still states there's no open project supporting Joule... 
Is there any entitlement that needs to be assigned to the subaccount or any other configuration to be performed beforehand?

BR Michael

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Public Cloud 公司代碼長度是否可以調整?
entry.content: Dear, 公司代码和公司的代码都是4位的。是系统设计，在数据库的字段也是这么设计的，无法更改的。 请问为什么需要更改呢。

entry.title: Re: In-House Repair in S/4HANA Service - Start with an Overview
entry.content:
Hi Sambit,

To integrate the Decide Actions in your system you need to use this IMG (SPRO) activity "Assign Action to SAP Fiori App." and maintain your Actions from your Action Profile of the In-House Service Transaction Type Action.

Best Regards,
Udo

entry.title: DTW- Outgoing Payment Upload - Journal Entry
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I want to upload an Outgoing Payment using the Data Transfer Workbench (DTW) in SAP Business One with the DocType set as Journal Entry. I am using two templates: Payment and Payment_Invoices.

However, since Journal Entries (JE) do not have a DocEntry field like AR and AP invoices, I encountered the following error when uploading:

"Invalid document number [VPM2.DocEntry][line: 1] Invalid document number [VPM2.DocEntry][line: 1]65171"

I have tried using TransId and Document No for the DocEntry field, but they did not work. Also, I couldn't find a DocEntry field in the OJDT table.

Is there any way to successfully upload the Outgoing Payment in this case? I would really appreciate your guidance.

Thank you!

entry.title: SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management Localization: Innovations in FPS3
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community!

Welcome back! In this edition we bring you the latest innovations and features in localization of SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition

Here we are talking about a comprehensive solution that handles subscription and usage-based models. It provides a standardized approach to recurring billing, invoicing, tax compliance, and automation.

With each release, SAP introduces localized country/region versions and new functions to assist customers with tax compliance, billing, invoicing, automation, and streamlined processes. This not only helps reduce compliance risks and costs but also improves the sustainability of tax operations.

For example, in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud FPS3 update, several countries and regions were incorporated into the SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management for localization.

For more information, I invite you to visit the following pages: 

  • Road Map Explorer: Future innovations planned 
  • SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management Localization on SAP Help Portal:  https://help.sap.com
  • What’s New: What's New Viewer - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Blog Post: SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management Localization: Sustainable Compliance for Your Recurring Revenue Business

The purpose of this post is to show you how SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management for SAP S/4HANA Localization is a game-changer for businesses looking to streamline their financial operations while ensuring compliance with local regulations. Stay tuned for the next update!

I hope you found this blog post interesting. If so, I invite you to follow my profile and the tags SAP Document and Reporting Compliance and SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management. Also, feel free to leave a comment or a suggestion in the comment section.

Thank you for choosing SAP to run your business complaint with local tax authorities!

entry.title: Re: Designing UI5 Apps as Business Solution for SAP Build Work Zone - Part 2
entry.content:
Hi @matheusog 

sorry for the delay, I hope you have found a solution in the meanwhile.
The launchpad does not support multiple apps in the subaccount, which means that a shared reuse app-host must not contain a manifest.json with sap.app/type: application. Could it be that "br.agr.fs.ct.motor.lib" is declared as application?
With the new CDM based approach you will no longer run into this limitation, as every business solution is completely separated from others.

Best regards
Matthias

entry.title: Re: Payment Method not viewable in APP Manage Supplier Line
entry.content: Hi Cristina,

entry.title: Re: Call an OData Web API Service externally
entry.content:
Hi Junwu,

Thank you for the respose.

Unfortunately, I don't have it in my URL.

entry.title: Unable to access "SAP Business Application Studio" after using it few days, throwing "Access Denied"
entry.content:
Unable to access "SAP Business Application Studio" after using it few days, throwing "Access Denied" suddenly.

I have necessary roles of BAS Administration as well as BAS developer roles to my account i logged in. I am using BTP trail account. Something got changed while trying to follow the golden path of https://pages.github.tools.sap/cap/golden-path/ for deployment production.

entry.title: Re: Problems with CAP App cds.persistence.skip
entry.content:
An entity annotated with `@cds.persistence.skip` or `@cds.persistence.exists` will not result in a database table being created for you. The `@cds.persistence.exists` annotation is mostly for scenarios, where you have an existing persistence which is outside of you CAP model. E.g. native HANA artifacts… Please read our documentation for more on that.

That being said, if you expose an entity with this annotation, you have to implement your own logic to e.g. READ data from this entity, which is not part of your CAP model.

entry.title: Re: SAP SD Sales Certification
entry.content:
Hello,

You can follow the guides here for the application procedure. You will be able to see the cost during the process.

SAP Certification support

Whether completing the quizzes on the SAP Learning Portal provides any discount is unknown to me. You need to figure it out yourself. 

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: S4HANA Value Summary for Aerospace & Defense
entry.content: Hi Williams43, the asset is 8 yrs old and the link has expired in the meantime. Please check the A&D website on sap.com on latest value drivers for A&D industry https://www.sap.com/industries/aerospace-defense.html or our new A&D whitepaper https://www.sap.com/documents/2016/03/32eb69d0-627c-0010-82c7-eda71af511fa.html. Best regards Thomas

entry.title: Re: SAP Replication flow from SAP Datasphere to S3
entry.content: If you want to only send deltas to the S3 bucket then you will need a transformation flow that sends only deltas to a new local table from the existing delta enabled table then another replication flow to send the deltas from the new table to AWS

entry.title: Help me with this code to display the FG code against each material code.
entry.content:
This is my abap code block

DATA: it_fg_code TYPE TABLE OF mast-matnr,   " Table to store FG Codes
      matnr1      TYPE mast-matnr.           " Variable for single FG Code

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK B1 WITH FRAME TITLE text-001.

SELECT-OPTIONS material FOR mast-matnr.
SELECT-OPTIONS plant FOR mast-werks.

SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK B1.

START-OF-SELECTION.

" Step 1: Fetch FG Code from MAST table for material type 'FERT'
SELECT DISTINCT mast~matnr
  INTO TABLE IT_MTYPE1
  FROM mast
  INNER JOIN mara ON mast~matnr = mara~matnr
  WHERE mast~matnr IN material
    AND mara~MTART = 'FERT'.

" Step 2: Fetch details from MAST, STPO, and MAKt for materials and their exploded parts
SELECT mast~werks
       mast~stlnr
       mast~matnr
       makt~maktx
       idnrk
       stpo~menge
  INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE itm_details
  FROM mast AS mast
  INNER JOIN stpo AS stpo ON mast~stlnr = stpo~stlnr
  INNER JOIN makt AS makt ON mast~matnr = makt~matnr
  WHERE mast~matnr IN material
    AND werks IN plant
    AND stpo~stlty = 'M'
    AND mast~stlal = mast~stlal.

IF NOT itm_details[] IS INITIAL.

  " Step 3: Fetch location data
  SELECT matnr
         werks
         lgpro
    INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE it_loc
    FROM marc
    FOR ALL ENTRIES IN itm_details
    WHERE werks = itm_details-werks
    AND matnr = itm_details-matnr.

ENDIF.

IF NOT itm_details[] IS INITIAL.

  " Step 4: Fetch verification data for the materials
  SELECT mast~matnr
         mast~werks
         verid
         bdatu
         adatu
         mkal~stlal
         bstma
         idnrk
    INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE it_ver
    FROM mast AS mast
    INNER JOIN mkal AS mkal ON mast~matnr = mkal~matnr
    INNER JOIN stpo AS stpo ON mast~stlnr = stpo~stlnr
    FOR ALL ENTRIES IN itm_details
    WHERE mast~werks = itm_details-werks
    AND mast~matnr = itm_details-matnr.

ENDIF.

* Step 5: Loop through itm_details and update FG Code for 'FERT' type materials
LOOP AT itm_details INTO DATA(ls_item).

  " Check if material type is 'FERT' by checking against IT_MTYPE1
  READ TABLE it_mtype1 INTO matnr1 WITH KEY matnr = ls_item-matnr.
  IF sy-subrc = 0.
    " Assign FG Code to the structure only for 'FERT' materials
    ls_item-fg_code = matnr1.  " Assign FG Code to the structure
  ELSE.
    " For exploded parts, set FG code to blank or empty
    ls_item-fg_code = ''.  " No FG code for exploded parts
  ENDIF.

The above mentioned code is displaying this output

I want on the FERT codes to be displayed against it.

entry.title: Re: Monitoring Integration Issues with the New “Issues Detected” Event in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Dear @rashmi_vanaja,

Good blog, and very detailed.  Thanks for taking the time to document it.

However, I am trying to understand the differences between:
* Erroneous XXXX Messages Detected (Single)
* Erroneous XXXX Messages Detected (Grouped)
* Issues Detected in XXXX Messages

(I am using XXXX because I'm working with IDocs)

Anyway, I did a lot of tests, and I cannot see any differences between the Metrics. I can change them, and they will behave the same way.

According to my test, those Metrics bring default parameters that give you the possibility to get a specific event, but If we change the parameters, the result will be the same.

For example, if I select"Erroneous XXXX Message Detected (Single), and I remove in "Grouping" tab, only the Message ID (that is unique), I have the same behavior as "Erroneous XXXX Message Detected (Grouped)" because there is no Message ID.

The Frequency of event calculation in the metric "Issues Detected in XXXX Messages" metric is the same as the Single or Grouped (depending on the parameters) because the job is the same that was set in /sdf/alm_setup (in our case every 5 minutes)

I would like to confirm this from your side, if you notice the same, because those metrics are listed as they are different, but are only like templates to start to configure your metrics.

Conclusion 1: I can use the "Erroneous XXXX Messages Detected (Single)" metric, and change all the parameters inside to get the same result as "Erroneous XXXX Message Detected (Grouped)".
Conclusion 2: I can use the "Erroneous XXXX Messages Detected (Single)" metric, and change the status group parameters inside to get the same result as "Issues Detected in XXXX Messages".

I would like to have a confirmation of this because I cannot see any different behavior in them.

Question: Why there are 3 different metrics? in the end, we can have the same result.

Thanks!

entry.title: RAP : Custom Entity usage of #FOR_INTENT_BASED_NAVIGATION
entry.content:
Dear Colleagues,

i have used #FOR_INTENT_BASED_NAVIGATION and used SemanticObjectAction = 'display'. When i open the Fiori app, it is diplaying all the semantic Actions for that object.

How can we control it only show the Action given like display in this case.

I tried with #WITH_INTENT_BASED_NAVIGATION but it is givinf duplicate element error. 

Best Regards,

Rohit

 
 
  •

After 4 hours to update ... need to restore backup...

entry.title: Configure File Path for Payment Medium | Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have successfully configured the DME and can download the file directly from our Public Cloud system using the Manage Payment Media app. However, our requirement is to automatically place the file in a specific file path on our local drive.

In SAP on-premise, we have the option to place the file path in payment medium creation configuration under the output control like this. (Attached the screenshot - 501646)

But this functionality cannot be seen in the public cloud system. (Attached Screenshot - Payment Medium)

We would like to know the available options in the Public Cloud landscape and assess the feasibility of specifying a file path within the system.

Thanks in Advance!

Dear All,
Can we install below software on Windows 2019 server:
01. SAP BI 4.2 SP08 patch07
02. SAP Lumira 2.4 Patch 2
03. SAP Data Services 4.2 SP14

Any help is apreciated

Best regards

entry.title: New Use Cases for SAP Connectivity in SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery
entry.content:
Developers today often face complex network topologies. You may be using a cloud service to run your continuous integration jobs, but important assets like source code or binary repositories could still be within your corporate network.

The SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery service was designed with this challenge in mind. From the beginning, we integrated the SAP Connectivity service to allow cloning of source code from on-premises repositories. Later, we added more features:

  • Connecting to on-premises Sonar code scanners
  • Deploying on ABAP systems 

Now, we have taken it one step further, enabling you to use the connectivity feature in your own scripts and configurations. As an example, I will describe how to use an on-premises npm registry.

But before we dive into that, I would like to explain how Connectivity is integrated into the Continuous Integration and Delivery service.

Connecting It All

When your continuous integration (CI) build interacts with the outside world, whether to fetch a source file, download a dependency, post a report, or perform other actions, this interaction generally involves sending an HTTP request.

If your request needs to reach an on-premises system, certain requirements must be met for Connectivity to work properly. Assuming that your target system is configured in your cloud connector, the request must:

  • Be sent via a specific proxy.
  • Contain custom HTTP headers, allowing Connectivity to authenticate and route the request.

However, not every tool allows for the setting of arbitrary HTTP headers. Therefore, we decided to use a proxy whose task is to accept HTTP requests, add the required headers, and forward the requests to the Connectivity service.

If your job is configured to use Connectivity (via a credential representing a Cloud Connector), this proxy will run in a container within the run environment of your build and wait for incoming requests. Note that this environment is not accessible from the outside world, ensuring that only your containers can send requests to the proxy.

Here is a diagram visualizing this setup:

Example: Using an On-Premises npm Registry

Enough theory; let's work through a real use case. Consider the following scenario:

We want to build a JavaScript application using npm. The problem is that the application has dependencies defined in its `package.json` which are located in an npm registry hosted by your company, and this registry is not accessible from the internet.

We'll assume for this scenario that all the dependencies can be fetched from that corporate registry. It is a common practice to have corporate registries that mirror registry.npm.org. This setup allows you to maintain copies of your open-source dependencies which you can then scan. Additionally, your build process no longer depends on the availability of registry.npm.org.

Let's go through the setup step by step.

Prerequisites

  • You have a Cloud Connector running in your corporate network.
  • You have a subscription of the SAP Continuous Integration and Deliver Service.

Prepare the Cloud Connector

  1. Add the account where the SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery service is subscribed to your Cloud Connector.
    This is described in the Cloud Connector Documentation.
  2. In the Cloud Connector, under "Cloud To On-Premise", add your private registry.
    The configuration should look something like this:

Configure the Continuous Integration and Delivery Service

In the UI of the service:

  1. Create a Cloud Connector Credential:

    First, create a Cloud Connector credential containing the location ID of your cloud connector. Name this credential connector.

  2. Create a Job for Building/Testing/Deploying Your Project:

    Next, create a job to build, test, and deploy your project. We want to use the on-premises registry during the build stage. To achieve this, add the connector credentials as an additional credential to that stage.

    This configuration will ensure that during the build execution, an environment variable CONN_PROXY will be available, holding the URL of the proxy.

    Example Build Stage:

  3. Configure Additional Variables:

    In addition, configure the following additional variables:

    • NPM_CONFIG_LOGLEVEL: info: This makes npm print more detailed information about what it is doing.
    • NPM_CONFIG_USERCONFIG: .cicd_npmrc: This makes npm use an npmrc file named .cicd_npmrc located in the project root.

    The last setting is particularly useful because it avoids altering the local build process of the project.

Prepare the Project

The only thing you need to do in the project is to add the npmrc file to be used in the central build. As mentioned above, the name should be .cicd_npmrc. Here is my file:

registry=http://on-prem-registry/
proxy=${CONN_PROXY}
replace-registry-host=always
 

Let's got through this line by line.

  • registry=http://on-prem-registry/: Here, I define the registry to be used. I refer to the registry by the virtual host name configured in the Cloud Connector. A port is not required because I used port 80, which is the standard port for the HTTP protocol. Note that I use HTTP and not HTTPS. This is due to a limitation of our current connectivity setup. However, it is not as bad as it sounds. Once the data reaches the proxy, it will be transmitted via an encrypted tunnel to the Cloud Connector.

  • proxy=${CONN_PROXY}: This defines that the proxy should be used. The actual URL is injected by the service. In practice, it will look like http://10.0.0.3:5003, where the port is mapped to the location ID of the Cloud Connector.

  • replace-registry-host=always: This is needed to avoid trouble with the package-lock.json files (in the project as well as in the dependencies). The package-lock.json is generated locally and contains registry URLs pointing to npm-registry.sap-dev.corp, the actual hostname of our internal registry. For the central build, another hostname (on-prem-registry) will be used, therefore the hostname has to be replaced.

That is all. Here is an excerpt from the build stage logs:

[2025-02-14T16:03:32.499Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - Discovered package.json file package.json
[2025-02-14T16:03:32.499Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - running command: npm config get registry -ws=false -iwr
[2025-02-14T16:03:32.500Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm info using npm@10.8.2
[2025-02-14T16:03:32.501Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm info using node@v20.17.0
[2025-02-14T16:03:32.501Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm info ok
[2025-02-14T16:03:32.501Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - Discovered pre-configured npm registry registry with value http://on-prem-registry/
[2025-02-14T16:03:32.501Z]
[2025-02-14T16:03:32.501Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - Running Install
[2025-02-14T16:03:32.501Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - running command: npm ci
[2025-02-14T16:03:32.762Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm info using npm@10.8.2
[2025-02-14T16:03:32.762Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm info using node@v20.17.0
[2025-02-14T16:03:33.688Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/@sap/xsenv/-/xsenv-4.2.0.tgz 725ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:33.688Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/@sap/e2e-trace/-/e2e-trace-4.1.0.tgz 766ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:33.948Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/@sap/logging/-/logging-7.1.0.tgz 910ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:34.204Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch POST 200 http://on-prem-registry/-/npm/v1/security/advisories/bulk 372ms
[2025-02-14T16:03:34.764Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/verror 739ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:34.764Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/ms 742ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:34.764Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/once 857ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:35.020Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/core-util-is 919ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:35.020Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/clone 932ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:35.020Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/wrappy 936ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:35.021Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/extsprintf 964ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:35.021Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/moment 967ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:35.021Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/once 973ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:35.021Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/assert-plus 983ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:35.021Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/next-line 988ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:35.021Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/next-line 986ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:35.021Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/wrappy 1025ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:35.022Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/wtfnode/-/wtfnode-0.9.1.tgz 1025ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:35.022Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/ws/-/ws-7.5.10.tgz 1026ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:35.022Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/request-stats 1030ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:35.022Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/yallist/-/yallist-2.1.2.tgz 1030ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:35.023Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/wrap-ansi/-/wrap-ansi-7.0.0.tgz 1036ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:35.023Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/wrappy/-/wrappy-1.0.2.tgz 1037ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:35.023Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/request-stats 1055ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:35.023Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/wrap-ansi/-/wrap-ansi-8.1.0.tgz 1051ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:35.023Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/which/-/which-2.0.2.tgz 1076ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:35.024Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/whatwg-url/-/whatwg-url-5.0.0.tgz 1076ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:35.024Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/winston-transport/-/winston-transport-4.9.0.tgz 1080ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:35.024Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/webidl-conversions/-/webidl-conversions-3.0.1.tgz 1080ms (cache miss)
 

As intended, the dependencies are being pulled from our internal registry.

Outlook and Next Steps

At this point, you should have realized that Connectivity can now be used in each stage and even in your custom scripting through the Additional Commands feature.

From Continuous Integration and Delivery side, the next Connectivity related feature we will take on is supporting the SOCKS protocol. This will eliminate the current restriction against using HTTPS and enable advanced scenarios such as combining mTLS with Connectivity.

entry.title: Re: No data was released for version Y10 and Consolidatio...
entry.content: Hi Paulo, thank you a lot. Did you solve the problem implementing this sap notes?

entry.title: Re: No data was released for version Y10 and Consolidatio...
entry.content: I don't understand this section: This problem can happen when there are two different consolidation unit attributes time and version dependent configuration at the same year.

entry.title: Re: Issue with outbound IDoc PEXR2003/PEXR2001 Missing segment and Unable to debug.
entry.content: The important question: did you use all the possible debug modes? (update task, user/external breakpoint instead of session breakpoints)

entry.title: Celebrating the Success of SAP Chota Talks – Hyderabad! 🎉
entry.content:
What an incredible day it was at SAP Chota Talks on February 22, 2025, hosted at School of Management Studies - University of Hyderabad.

This event was all about knowledge sharing, community engagement, and inspiring discussions around SAP and its evolving ecosystem.

Organizers, Volunteers & Speaker's

A huge shoutout to all the speakers, attendees, and supporters who made this event a grand success! Your enthusiasm and passion for SAP created an amazing learning environment, fostering innovation and collaboration.
From insightful sessions to thought-provoking discussions, SAP Chota Talks truly highlighted the strength of the SAP Community in India. Events like these remind us how powerful it is when like-minded professionals come together to exchange ideas and grow together.

Detailed Blog was written by @MaheshCV SAP CHOTA TALKS Hyderabad 2025 (Q1) - Event High... - SAP Community along with the presentation details 

 Let's keep the momentum going and continue to build a stronger, more connected SAP ecosystem! 🚀

entry.title: Re: No data was released for version Y10 and Consolidatio...
entry.content: This problem can happen when there are two different consolidation unit attributes time and version dependent configuration at the same year.What operation I have to do about that?

entry.title: Re: Mass uploader for stock correction
entry.content:
Hi @PranavaM ,

Seems you need to develop a custom Program/Tcode using Standard Tcode ADGI...example Pass the stock info into selection screen...then do Addition or subtraction of stock if quant (Material,batch...) available...

Please let us know if any further questions.

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Re: Mass creation of assets
entry.content: Is there a similar solution available for S/4 HANA On-Premise?

Hi Expert:

 

the item category D is active by default in SAP back end but on Fiori not show item category = D.

anywhere to activate this.materi

entry.title: Re: How to check not allocate amount after material ledge...
entry.content: thank you very much!

entry.title: Re: Joule speaks ABAP!
entry.content:
Hello @AlexanderRother 

According to Note 3571857 we, as a sap partner should get in touch with TDD@sap.com for acquiring the additional licence 8019124. But we got this answer:

8019124 - Joule for developer limited promotion, ABAP - Due to technical issues Joule is currently not available for ordering under TDD. We expect these technical issues to be resolved soon but cannot commit to a specific timeline.

Please look out for any news published on the TDD AI Info page.

 

Is there any info when this is available for partners? Next weeks/Months? Bc this service is only available until september.

Greetings
Jan

entry.title: Re: 🧰👷‍♂️ The SAP S/4HANA RISE & SAP BTP - Toolbox 👷‍♂️🧰
entry.content:
so..... What are SAP AI Agents ?   What are AI agents: Benefits and business applications | SAP

 

Joule Collaborative AI Agents to Transform How People Work | SAP News Center

Joule Agents collaborating across Business Functions and Processes

SAP Joule Agents collaborating across complex functions...

SAP Generative AI overview page Generative AI | SAP Artificial Intelligence Innovations

What Is SAP AI Core? | SAP Help Portal

Learning Journey - Solving Your Business Problems Using Prompts and LLMs in SAP's Generative AI Hub

Reference Architecture Retrieval Augmented Generation and Generative AI on the SAP BTP SAP Discovery Center Architecture - Retrieval Augmented Generation and Generative AI on SAP BTP

entry.content:
Hi @Mandy2025,

This might be related to missing access rights in your system. Do you have the Extensibility Specialist and Configuration Expert - Business Process Configuration roles assigned to your user?

Kind regards,
Ana

entry.title: Re: No data was released for version Y10 and Consolidatio...
entry.content: Hi Vanessa. Yes, Implementing the SAP note solved the problem

entry.title: Re: Area of responsibility - APP Setting/MRP Setting access in Fiori
entry.content:
Please can someone help and advise me on how to mass Assign the AOR (Area of Responsibility) in Fiori to all users on MASS. We don't need to assign individual AOR's. We want to apply all AOR's to all users as a default.   It is very frustrating to expect each user to assign the AOR's for Production Supervisor, Work Centre, MRP Controller Codes.  We have hundreds to scroll through.  Surely there must be an option to default the ALL ASSIGNED for all users.

thanks

entry.title: Re: EH&S: import of phrases with CG3Z
entry.content:
Hi 

Could you please share the excel file format for loading the Phrases

entry.title: Error: Fiori tool guided development with OData service v2
entry.content:
Hello,

I recentely found the sap fiori tools: guided deveopment does not work with Odata service V2 in VS Code and BAS. Do you have any solution for that? 

(It works well with the odata V4 )

Version:

Sap fiori tools: 1.16.4

VS code: 1.97.2

nodeJs:20.18.1

Thanks in advance,

SAP Fiori tools SAP Business Application Studio #visual studio code

entry.title: Re: Deleting Bank vendor data using class VMD_EI_API
entry.content:
Hello,

I managed this by setting the flag current_state to 'X' and the bankdetails task type to 'I'. This is basically telling the system to ignore the current master data situation and just save whatever information you are passing in the current call. If you need to delete all the data, just pass an empty table.

entry.title: Re: Efficient Data Retrieval using Pagination in SAP BTP IS/CI with HTTP Adapter
entry.content: At the end of the local integration process, we need to store the XML records in the datastore.  In the next Iflow When retrieving the collected data from the datastore, we can set the maximum number of polling messages to ensure all data is retrieved in a single file, which can then be sent to the receiver.

entry.title: Re: SAC USER ID
entry.content:
Hi, @William_Yu 

thank you very much for your response.

But if I enable SSO and select USER ID under Choose a user attribute to map your Identity Providers - then the user is identified by the USER ID, and the USER ID must match the one in the IdP. Or is that not correct?

Thank you very much

Best Regards

entry.title: S/4 Hana On Premise version 2023 - Report s_pl0_090004 Withholding Tax Return - Portugal
entry.content:
Dear Gurus,

In my Withholding Tax Return (tcode S_PL0_090004), the system does not select vendor documents (invoices) with withholding tax types that are posted at the time of payment. Instead, it only includes the corresponding payment documents in the report.

I have already reviewed SAP Note 3351782, but my configuration appears to be correct. Do you know what could be causing this behavior?

Please find attached two screenshots: one showing the vendor invoices (which are correct) and another showing only the payment documents.

Could you please assist me with this?

Thank you.

entry.content: I'm definitely not on time here, but your answer cleared a lot of air. Thanks!

Thank you for your assistance.

 

Transaction Type
310000001
20
60
67
59
21
19
18
202
15
10000071
69

entry.title: Let's start your Synthetic User Monitoring journey
entry.content:
This blog is the first of a series that will take you on a journey on Synthetic User Monitoring in SAP Cloud ALM.

In this blog, you will learn what Synthetic User Monitoring looks like and how it can help you proactively monitor the performance and the availability of your web applications from the perspective of its end-users.

Concept

Synthetic User Monitoring (SUM) monitors your web application by simulating the actions of a typical user who accesses it. 

When a user accesses a web application, he typically:

  1. Starts a web browser,
  2. Opens the URL of the portal that gives access to the application (like the SAP Launchpad or directly the application URL)
  3. Authenticates. E.g. type a user name, a password, then click a logon button
  4. Clicks on a tile
  5. Waits for the application to start.
  6. Perform some operations
  7. Finally, logs out

With SUM, you can define such a sequence of actions and regularly replay it from different monitoring locations representing the main users' locations.

From these replays, SUM evaluates:

  • The availability: was the sequence executed without problem?
  • The performance: did the execution take more time than usually? (using the SUM Dynamic Thresholds)

These metrics are stored for a configurable amount of time.
With that, you can answer questions like:

  • Is my application available for its users?
  • Does its availability (or performance) evolve over the time? or at some moment of the day?
  • Does its availability differs depending on where it is accessed from (location)?
  • ...

You can also configure SUM to automatically perform an action when it detects a problem.
For instance:

  • Create an alert,
  • Send an email,
  • Create an incident, 
  • ...

The SUM scenarios

In Synthetic User Monitoring, the sequence of actions is a named: SUM Scenario.

It is based on a Script that you describe using Selenium IDE (an open source widely used by many companies).

SUM Scenario steps

In the Selenium IDE script, SUM gives you the possibility to split the sequence of actions in functional Steps:

  • authentication,
  • navigation to the application,
  • ...,
  • logout.

SUM will then compute intermediate availability and performance metrics for each step.

The SUM runners

In Synthetic User Monitoring, the monitoring location is represented by a SUM Runner.

A runner can be any Selenium Remote web driver infrastructure.

This is where the web browser is started. So this is where SUM captures the measures to determine the availability and the performance.

Overview of the application

Note: the configuration and the details will be covered in the following blogs of this series.

Overview page

When you enter the application, you land on its Overview page.

This page shows you the current situation of the Scenarios you selected in your scope.

For each scenario, you see in a glimpse:

  • What are its current availability and performance statuses per monitoring location.
  • How many executions finished in which status during the last one hour (this time frame is configurable), 

Executions page

You want more details? You can click on the Scenario tile to access the Executions page.

Here, you can see the evolution of the executions statuses for the last one hour.
You can also see the evolution of the performance and the response time.

If one hour is not enough, or if you want to focus on an older period of time, use the Time Frame selector to change the considered time frame.
SUM keeps up to 30 days of executions information.

You can also see, in parallel, the executions of some other scenarios.

Executions details

If you want more details about an execution, you can click on the execution marker.
This drills down to the Execution Details.
Here, you can:

  • Understand the reasons for the execution status: which step shows performance or availability issue,

  • Check the screenshots automatically captured during the scenario execution.
    (this requires to activate the screenshots capture in the scenario configuration),

  • And, in the Metrics section, understand which action took the more time.

Statistics

If you want to see if an issue is recurrent, Synthetic User Monitoring includes some statistics views.
From the Execution page, you can quickly see the evolution of a scenario metrics over different predefined time frames:

  • Availability,

  • Performance,

  • Response time.

You can also use the Statistics page to easily compare this evolution:

  • Across the scenarios in your scope,

  • And across the locations where a scenario is activated.


SAP Cloud ALM Analytics API

If you need more custom analytics views, you can use the SAP Cloud ALM Analytics API to export the SUM metrics to your preferred dashboard tool: SAP Analytics Cloud, Grafana, ...

For more information, you can visit the Analytics API section of the API Guide for SAP Cloud ALM.

Here is an example of a Grafana dashboard consuming SUM metrics:

Alerts page

If you have configured Synthetic User Monitoring to emit events when a problem is detected, you can see the current alerts on the Alerts page.

Additional monitoring capabilities

And if you already have something in place that regularly captures availability and response time metrics, you can use the SAP Cloud ALM External API to regularly send these metrics in Synthetic User Monitoring.

This API gives you the possibility to map your metrics with the SUM concepts of Scenarios and Runners.

Once in Synthetic User Monitoring, you will benefit for most of the SUM capabilities:

  • Display the current status,
  • Display statistics about the evolution of your metrics,
  • Evaluate the performance based on the SUM Dynamic Thresholds,
  • Benefit for SAP Cloud ALM events capabilities: create alerts, send notifications, create incidents, ...
  • ...

All in a same tool; along with the statuses of your regular SUM scenarios

For more information, you can visit the Raw Data Inbound Traces API section of the API Guide for SAP Cloud ALM.

 

You now have an overview of what you can do with Synthetic User Monitoring in SAP Cloud ALM.
Stay tuned for the next stop on your Synthetic User Monitoring journey.
I will incorporate here the links to next articles.

In the meantime, if you want to experience Synthetic User Monitoring by yourself, you can access the SAP Cloud ALM - Public Demo Tenant.

Divya

entry.title: Re: Celebrating the Success of SAP Chota Talks – Hyderabad! 🎉
entry.content:
Hi There,

Did you missed SAP CHOTA TALKS Hyderabad (Q1) 2025 , Make sure its on your radar for the next one - you wont want to miss these game changing insights/discussions.

entry.title: Do you know how to use the reporting options in SAP Digital Manufacturing?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Analyzing Manufacturing Data in SAP Digital Manufacturing Insights learning journey.

Overview
This course aims at business users and consultants, who want to analyze the manufacturing data. The reporting options in SAP Digital Manufacturing are introduced which ensure traceability and generate insights to understand the root-causes of losses on the shop floor. 

Learning objectives

  • Overview about Digital Manufacturing for Insights
  • Understand how to collect manufacturing data and how to create a data collection concept
  • Managing Operational Reports
  • Describing Live Monitoring with Line Monitor POD
  • Building the Overall Equipment Efficiency (OEE) KPI
  • Utilizing the Embedded SAP Analytics Cloud (eSAC)

Prerequisites

  • Required:
    Discovering SAP Digital Manufacturing
  • Recommended/Optional:
    Executing Processes for Discrete Industries in SAP Digital Manufacturing

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: #COE#RFL01-Some SSCUI are displayed in Quality and not in Production
entry.content:
Dear All, 

We're still discovering SCCUIs that are displayed and accessible in quality by the App  Manage your solution, but that can't be seen in manage your solution in production.

For example the SSCUI 100066 we can display the SSCUI with the app manage your solution in quality system, BUT we can not display the SSCUI with the app manage your solution in Production system. 

Does anyone know how can we have a complete list of SSCUI that are visible in Qual and not in prod, so that we don't have to check roles every time? 

I've searched for notes and haven't found any answer to this question.

Thank you very much for your help. 

Best regards, 

Mouna

#SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance

entry.title: Re: Mass uploader for stock correction
entry.content:
Hi Ajit,

Thank you for your response could you elaborate on how this can be done for SAP S4HANA Cloud Public edition?

entry.title: Re: Massive add of language key in gl account SAP PUBLIC...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for your question.

Yes, this is something that you can do in the G/L Account Renumbering process.

You can download the corresponding the relevant G/L accounts to a spreadsheet, add the translations, and then upload the spreadsheet again.

Here are more details: How to Renumber G/L Accounts.

Best regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Upload Prices in Sales from template-based spreadsheet

entry.title: Distribution of amount in AUC Settlement rule
entry.content:
Hi All, 

when we are going to settle the AUC to Main Asset, We want to settle these AUC to main Asset based on amount rather than number.  But Amount option is not available in AIAB.

Can we settle AUC to main Assets based on AMOUNT BY AIAB transection . IF Yes  then HOW ??

entry.title: Re: Transaction Type OF OINM TABLE
entry.content:
Hello,

This column is related with the object type. Check this link for the complete list:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/list-of-object-types-sap-business-one/ba-p/13336072

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Alerts not working after upgrade to version 10
entry.content: If the service is not responding, try to reboot the server. Best regards

entry.title: Re: how to create time-based Condition Monitoring Plan
entry.content: i was unable to do what you told. can you please elaborate?

entry.title: Re: Transporting content in SAP BUILD Process Automation
entry.content:
Hello @wvillam,

Here an answer from cTMS point of view, but hope this will help nevertheless. In principle, cTMS can also handle transports between Global Accounts and across data centers (regions) for most content types, as long as you can set up a destination that can reach the other account (exception: it is NOT possible to transport SAP BTP ABAP content across global accounts), as listed in SAP Cloud Transport Management | DevOps | SAP Community.

Nevertheless, trial accounts are restricted accounts and I have also experienced issues with connectivity in the past, so I assume that it might really not be working to transport between two trial accounts, as they might not "see" each other. Unfortunately, I could not find a detailed description of the restrictions that are in place for trial accounts.

To try out transports in general, depending on the scenario, you could try to create several subaccounts within the same trial account or use several spaces, although you might face issues with restricted quota and unfortunately, I do not have details how it looks like for SAP Build Process Automation.

Best regards,
Boris

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 Date Picker only for Month and Year
entry.content: This issue is related to the version you are using. For example, I tried it with 1.44.39, and it didn't work. Then I upgraded the version, and it worked with 1.71.63.

entry.title: Re: Looking for Late Payment interest and penalties repor...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for your question.

Can you please have a look at this information on the SAP Help Portal?

Displaying the Risk of Late Payment for Your Invoices 

Predictive Scenario for Risk of Late Payment 

Determination of Interest and Charges 

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Functional Highlights of the New 3.0 FP02 Release of PLM System Integration for SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Dear PLM community,

two months into the new year, we are happy to offer you a new feature pack of PLM System Integration for SAP S/4HANA. Our engineering teams did their best to deliver a wealth of new features and improvements that can help you enhance system integration and streamline your business processes.

As part of this 3.0 FP02 release, you can use a new Domain Model entity to create Manufacturing Reference Structures, leverage new integration capabilities for SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for extended production engineering and operations (PEO), enjoy improved list handling for production operations, and use additional material master views. Furthermore, our new BAdIs, custom logic capabilities, and data cleanup processes can help you achieve greater efficiency and effectiveness in your SAP operations. Read on to find out all the details that will help you make the most of the new features. 

 

Create Manufacturing Reference Structures

PLM system integration for SAP S/4HANA now supports the creation of basic manufacturing reference structures via a new Domain Model entity View Structure Version (VSV). You can transfer source structures from an external product lifecycle management (PLM) system and use them for manufacturing processes. The integration can create SAP PEO MRS objects, MRS structure items, and BOM links.

 

Realize Manufacturing Process Definition Scenarios for SAP PEO

PLM system integration for SAP S/4HANA now allows you to set up a manufacturing process definition scenario for SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for production engineering and operations: Transfer variable production operation lists for configurable routings that support logical component allocations and provide the manufacturing engineering department with all the information they need.

Transfer Work Instructions From an External PLM system

You can also use our integration to transfer work instructions that were authored in an external product lifecycle management (PLM) system to the production integration portal of SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for extended production engineering and operations. This will help you improve your master data quality as well as transparency across different domains by sharing information between the engineering department and the extended supply chain.

 

Enjoy Improved List Handling in Production Operations

Leverage advanced list handling capabilities for production operations as part of the routing transfer, such as parallel operation constellation and other effectivity information. Enable the parallelization of sequences for classic routing scenarios. Based on a POL, you can create a classic PP routing in the SAP system, supporting a default mode with only a standard sequence with one predecessor per operation. For more advanced routing, a sequence-based mode allows you to use standard, parallel, and alternative sequences, while a heuristic-based mode automatically determines standard and parallel sequences based on operation predecessors.

 

 Profit from Additional Material Master Views

Extend the creation of material master data beyond basic and plant-specific views. With this new release, you can create views such as default sales or quality management views, from scratch or based on specific view templates. Leveraging BRF+, these views help you to improve master data quality and enhance communication between engineering and the extended supply chain.

 

What Else is New in PLM system integration for SAP S/4HANA?

Besides the innovations above, we further improved your trusted PLMSI add-on to deliver you a range of new functions. Enjoy!

Enhanced Integration Capabilities thanks to New BAdIs and Custom Logic

We have introduced several new Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) that provide greater flexibility and customization options. Use additional extension fields, optimize your customer order item processing, and leverage new methods for defining custom logic that allow for more precise handling of inbound and outbound requests. For detailed information, see our What's New Viewer.

Streamlined Data Cleanup Processes

The supported data cleanup processes have been streamlined for better usability. Access a comprehensive group of data cleanup functions from just one report, including the deletion of obsolete migration data, selected key mappings or PLMSI messages and logs, and the cleaning up orphaned key mappings. This consolidation simplifies the data cleanup process and ensures that all relevant tasks are performed efficiently.

 

Improved Usability of the PLMSI Workbench

The usability of the PLMSI Workbench has been significantly enhanced with new functions. Users can now edit requests of messages that have not been processed directly on the Request tab, call up aggregated logs for selected tasks and subtasks, and navigate to the source code of an object directly from the object list of a processing step. Furthermore, you can navigate directly to PLM system integration Customizing or initiate data cleanup.

 

 

Business Content

As for each feature pack, we have updated our comprehensive set of business content (BC) sets to help you configure your system with ease. For details, see Initial Configuration.

For a comprehensive list of our new and enhanced features for release 3.0 FP02, check out our What's New Viewer.

Expand Your Range

With our past release, we had expanded the range of external PLM systems you can connect to your SAP system using PLM system integration. With this release, we proudly announce that our partner product PLM system integration, add-on for 3DEXPERIENCE has already published its second release, 3.1. Check out the Release Notes to learn about latest updates.

All relevant information on PLM system integration is also available via the links below.

  • SAP Help Portal
    • Documentation of PLM system integration for SAP S/4HANA

 SAP Product Availability Matrix (SAP login required):

    • Matrix for PLM system integration 3.0 for SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP Roadmap Explorer:
    • SAP Roadmap for PLM system integration

And last but not least – contact us via our excellent forum if you have any questions.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest Winners Revealed
entry.content:
Congrats to the winners, I wish you all a journey full of leraning and enjoyment.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Changing pre-delivered master data in public cloud (F...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question!

Here's some helpful information:

Define FS Items 

Defining Subitems

Breakdown Categories

Document Types and Posting Levels

Define Balance Validation Tasks

The Group Reporting documentation is available here.

If the content delivered by SAP doesn't fulfil your needs, you can create your own content.

Kind regards,
Henrike

entry.title: Re: Distribution of amount in AUC Settlement rule
entry.content: Hi, Please go to Menu Card and check Environment >Asset Values .

entry.title: Re: Mass uploader for stock correction
entry.content:

I din't find any solution to obtain this result.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Cummulative quantities for scheduling agreement

Hello,

Thank you for your question.

Can you please have a look at the following information?

FAQ for Sales Scheduling Agreements: Cumulative Quantities

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: How to connect Power Automate from Dynamics 365 to SAP for order fulfillment with custom fields?
entry.content:
Hey everyone,

I’m trying to set up an automated system using Power Automate. I want to connect Dynamics 365 with SAP for order fulfillment and to update our inventory. The plan is to automatically send order details to SAP when an order is created or updated. Then, I want to keep inventory levels updated in both systems.

I’m running into issues with some custom fields we added in Dynamics 365. I need to make sure these fields match up correctly with the ones in SAP. I want everything to run smoothly.

Has anyone done something like this? I’d love to hear how you mapped your custom fields and kept inventory updates happening in real-time. Any tips on how to tackle this or things to watch out for would be really helpful!

Thanks a lot for your help!

Ivan Farafonov

HireDynamicsDevelopers

entry.title: Re: Hi All - Where can i find a list of training and Cert...
entry.content:
Hi @IkEgh 
In addition to @JanSchlichting 's reply I recommend to check the learning material we provide our SAP Learning platform. To expand your horizons and explore new opportunities to get the most from your work access the Learn by role site.
Business User how want to learn how to design business rules and processes and improve SAP products and solutions find learning material here.
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Using OData in a Ui-Integration Card (Component)
entry.content:
Hi,

I managed to get a Component type card working with a destination!
First we would be exposing the card directly to the Component.js. This would allow us to do requests via the card directly

// Follow the first link and adjust Component.js so you can access the card directly from the Controller

  var oCard = this.getOwnerComponent().card;
 // Here we do the request based on the official documentation
            oCard.request({
                "url": "{{destinations.Destination}}/.../YourDestination",
                "parameters": {
                    "$format": "json"
                }
            }).then(function (aData) {
              // this is your data in the controller
            });
 
Hope this is helpful
Sebastian

entry.title: AI in Onboarding
entry.content:
This article was written together with @Angela_Romano and @kkhullar.

This blog provides an overview of the AI features available in Onboarding as of 2H 2024 release.

AI Assisted Writing in Onboarding

The AI-assisted writing tool is an interactive tool that uses generative AI capabilities to assist your employees with their writing tasks. You can leverage this feature to enhance your writing in Onboarding. 

This feature allows you to:

  • Enhance your writing by suggesting enhancements in your writing for example grammar, spelling.
  • Change the tone of the content. For example to make it more professional.
  • Make your content shorter or longer
  • Create bulleted lists
  • Analyze text to detect bias, and suggest replacement text
  • Translate text into local languages

As of 2H 2024 release, AI Assisted Writing is supported for the following areas in Onboarding 

  • Welcome Message 
  • Farewell message
  • Buddy message
  • Goal content

AI Assisted Writing feature for Welcome Message

 

To enable this feature, you will need the following:

1. SAP AI Units license (engage your account manager to find details on this).

2. Add AI Services Admin permission for your system administrator role.

3. Navigate to AI Services Admin and enable the Assisted Writing with Text analyzer under Global features 

4. Enable your users ( for example Managers) to use this feature by adding the permission Assisted Writing with Text Analyzer

For additional info on Generative AI please review the blog written by @jenny_geipel 

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-sap/successfactors-ai-ai-assisted-writing-and-writing-translation-set-up-and/ba-p/13988897

entry.title: Re: Aging Report for Accounts Payable not working
entry.content: Do not use Answer to respond to a comment or answer. Answer is intended to be used to propose a solution to the initial question. Use Comment on the initial question or on any answer instead.

entry.title: Re: Assign multiple valuation methodes in FCV to one Valiualtion rule

entry.title: Re: Aging Report for Accounts Payable not working
entry.content: Are you the admin, and did you apply the steps described in the KBA. Or did you ask your Admin?

entry.content:

Regards,
Christoph

entry.title: Re: Deployment Scheduling in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Great news!
One question. The note from the Article: “Changing private project authorizations after you created a schedule doesn't have any effects on the deployment schedule”

Use case: the Project Manager decided to restrict authorizations User#1 to view schedules only. So, logically, that means the User#1 won’t be able to change the schedules. But in accordance the design the changes still changeable by User#1.
Hence, someone, User#2, should track these kind of authorization changes and re-create these “past” schedules to avoid changing the schedules by User#1.
As I got this note correctly?
I anticipate questions from security audits on this issue. And I don't want to waste my colleagues' time checking authorisation after a team member's authorisation has been changed.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Continuous Quality Check Services for SAP IBP Implementation
entry.content:
Embarking on a project can be both exciting and daunting. One of the key aspects of a successful implementation is ensuring the quality and stability of your solution. SAP Enterprise Support offers continuous quality checks to support this goal. 

What are Continuous Quality Checks?

Continuous quality checks (CQC) are remote services that analyze your system and/or specific situations to help reduce system risks and outline optimization potential. You will receive a report, including an executive summary with findings and guidance for improvement.

Continuous Quality Checks for SAP IBP

To support your SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain (SAP IBP) implementation project, the following continuous quality check services are available:

  1. CQC for Transformation Assessment for SAP IBP - Prepare* — The latest CQC service offering that helps review the project team’s awareness of key SAP IBP topics, ensuring that you are in the right direction at the early stage of the project.
  2. CQC Going Live Support — To help you through a smooth start of production.
  3. CQC Deployment Readiness — For a detailed analysis of your SAP IBP before your go live.
  4. CQC Business Process Performance Optimization (CQC BPPO) — For a performance analysis of your business critical process steps for optimal performance improvement.

Requesting Remote Services

The diagram below highlights the recommended timings for requesting the CQC services, aligned with the SAP Activate Methodology phases, from Discover to Run. 

For information about CQC services for supply chain management, including how to request them, visit the Continuous Quality Checks* section of our Supply Chain Management Solutions value map*.

*To access the link and for ongoing support and actionable guidance tailored to your unique SAP journey, we invite you to join and explore SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps. 

Get Moving with SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps

Included with every SAP cloud subscription, value maps offer structured, product-level Learning Rooms where you can get personalized guidance for your SAP IBP journey.

Access Value Maps in 3 Easy Steps:

  1. Sign up for SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support (please allow 2 hours for processing).
  2. Access value maps via the Learning Hub.
  3. Navigate to the SAP Enterprise Support value maps for Supply Chain Management Solutions. Once in the value maps, you can collaborate in forums, ask questions, or “Request a Call” to speak with one of our experts for further assistance.

Value maps are intuitive to navigate, but we recommend scheduling an onboarding call to tailor the experience to your specific needs and highlight the most relevant content.

Got a question about value maps or our content?

  • Use the “ask questions” feature in the value maps.
  • Or, contact us at: sapesvaluemaps@sap.com

Brought to you by the SAP Enterprise Support value map team for Supply Chain Management Solutions.

entry.title: Re: Row Level Security in SAC
entry.content: Thank you for your continuous support, we really appreciate it. Unfortunately, we tried this approach but it didn't work yet, we created a group that contained one user as a test but when we give the read access to the group team they still can't see the data, but once we give the same access to the user itself the data is shown just fine, are we missing somethin?

entry.title: selecting Multiple elements in "Set looping list" in bpa
entry.content:
In the set looping list, we can choose either of these elements:

But do we have any alternative to select all the three collections of elements under the mobiles screen so that only one For each loop can be used to get the data of all the three elements in this screen

 

SAP Build Process Automation SAP Intelligent Robotic Process Automation

entry.title: Why hint doesn't work?
entry.content:
Hi, experts. Can someone explain why mine hint makes even worse? And how i can optimize and speed up my query?

I've have 160 sec without hint and 180 with it.

data: lt_displace_alv type table of zts_displacex_dev,
ls_displace_alv like line of lt_displace_alv,
ls_displace_alv1 like line of lt_displace_alv.

SELECT b~bstyp bsart a~ebeln b~ebelp
matnr txz01 meins lmein b~menge
lgort reslo eindt wamng wemng
werks reswk lifnr umrez umren elikz eglkz
FROM ekko AS a
INNER JOIN ekpo AS b
ON a~ebeln = b~ebeln
INNER JOIN eket AS c
ON b~ebeln = c~ebeln
AND b~ebelp = c~ebelp
INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE lt_displace_alv
where b~werks = 1000
and b~loekz eq space
AND NOT EXISTS ( SELECT ebeln FROM mseg WHERE ebeln = b~ebeln ) .
* %_HINTS DB6 '<IXSCAN TABLE=''MSEG'' INDEX=''"KEKO~ZCO"'' />'.
WRITE '123'.
